perturbation.original_text: Party B shall bear any consequences and legal liability arising out of Party B's non-compliance, and Party A shall have no liability. Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement without further legal liability in the case that Party B does not comply with Party A's management and arrangement.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall bear any consequences and legal liability arising out of Party B's non-compliance, and Party A shall have no liability.
perturbation.explanation: Removing Party A's right to terminate the agreement without legal liability creates an inconsistency. While Party B is still liable for non-compliance, the clause that explicitly allows Party A to terminate the contract without legal repercussions is now omitted. This creates uncertainty about Party A's termination rights for non-compliance, contradicting the implicit understanding that Party A has some recourse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement if Party B has breached the above articles in this Agreement. The termination of this Agreement shall not prejudice Party A's right to hold Party B responsible for breach of contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly granted Party A the right to terminate the agreement without further legal liability if Party B didn't comply with Party A's management. The changed text removes this right. The contradicted text states that Party A can terminate the agreement if Party B breaches the agreement, and this termination does not prejudice Party A's right to hold Party B responsible for the breach of contract. By removing Party A's explicit right to terminate without liability in the case of non-compliance as described in the original text, and with the contradicted text stating that termination does not prejudice Party A's right to hold Party B responsible for breach of contract, this creates a strong contradiction regarding the conditions and consequences of terminating the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of parcel damage resulting from leakage or fire, Party B shall indemnify at the standard rate of RMB200 per parcel, and indemnify the actual price for high-end insured parcel (or indemnify by the value of the parcel provided by arbitration department determined by Party A).
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of parcel damage resulting from leakage or fire, Party B shall indemnify at a rate determined by Party A.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific indemnification rate of RMB200 per parcel and the clarification regarding high-end insured parcels, the contract now provides only that Party B will indemnify at a rate determined by Party A, which creates an In-Text Contradiction with the later text referencing the original indemnification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any parcel damage resulting from the fault of Party B's employees shall be compensated by Party B in accordance with Party A's relevant rules. Party B shall be responsible for all of Party A's economic loss and relevant liquidated damages arising out of any theft and disposal of stolen goods conducted by Party B's personnel. Such payment shall be deducted from the current month's freight and be topped up by Party B in case of inadequacy. The personnel breaching the rules shall be dismissed by Party B.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a fixed indemnification rate (RMB200 per parcel) and provided a clarification for high-end insured parcels. The changed text removes this specificity, stating that the rate will be 'determined by Party A.' This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which refers to Party B compensating parcel damage according to 'Party A's relevant rules.' The changed text removes the reference to the original indemnification rate, leading to a contradiction because the original compensation agreement does not exist anymore. Since the old fixed rate is now removed, this impacts the compensation expected from Party B based on party A's rules.

perturbation.original_text: Party B shall arrive at the network partners determined by Party A according to the time and route stipulated in this Agreement, and strictly comply with the start time and end time. Unless otherwise approved by Party A, in the event of parcel transfer due to Party B's vehicle delay, Party B shall pay liquidated damages to Party A at the standard rate of RMB500 per trip on the first working day, RMB1,000 per trip on the second working day and RMB2,000 per trip on the third working day (based on the time records on the parcel transfer documents) and such liquidated damages will be deducted from the current month's freight. In the special event of changes to route and time and severe weather (or other force majeure events), Party B shall be in touch with Party A in time and record such special event on the parcel transfer documents with Party A's approval.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall arrive at the network partners determined by Party A according to the time and route stipulated in this Agreement, and strictly comply with the start time and end time. In the special event of changes to route and time and severe weather (or other force majeure events), Party B shall be in touch with Party A in time and record such special event on the parcel transfer documents with Party A's approval.
perturbation.explanation: The omission removes the specific liquidated damages Party B must pay for delays, which creates an In-Text Contradiction when considering the overall intention of the contract, where a structure of fines and remuneration is created. The rest of the text now lacks the clarity for delay fines, especially how and when those fines will be deducted, making enforcement difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B shall obtain Party A's written consent in the case the early termination of the Agreement. Party B shall pay one-month freight as liquidated damages in case of termination of the Agreement without consent. Within the contract period, Party B shall not charge the freight difference if Party A rents same-level vehicles. Party B shall also compensate Party A's other losses.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the specific liquidated damages in the Changed Text creates a direct contradiction with the principle of liquidated damages outlined in the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text mentions liquidated damages related to early termination of the agreement, indicating an intention to apply financial penalties for breaches. By removing the specific liquidated damages related to vehicle delays, the Changed Text creates uncertainty and inconsistency in the application of penalties, as the specific instances of penalties are no longer directly defined in the contract. This inconsistency directly conflicts with the principle established in the Contradicted Text, making the contract less clear and potentially unenforceable regarding delays. Also, based on the Perturbation Explanation, there is an In-Text Contradiction when considering the overall intention of the contract, where a structure of fines and remuneration is created. The rest of the text now lacks the clarity for delay fines, especially how and when those fines will be deducted, making enforcement difficult.
